sections.text: Imagine you want to send a letter to your friend who lives far away. You write the letter, put it in an envelope, write their address on it, and drop it in a mailbox. A postman picks it up, carries it across the country, and delivers it to your friend's door. The web works almost exactly like this — except instead of letters, we send web pages, and instead of postmen, we have computers!

sections.title: The Big Picture
sections.text: The web is a huge collection of pages — like a gigantic library — stored on special computers called servers all around the world. When you want to see a page, your computer (the client) asks the server for it, and the server sends it back. This all happens in less than a second!

sections.title: Think of it like a Pizza Shop 🍕
sections.text: You (the client) call a pizza shop (the server) and say 'I want a pepperoni pizza!' (a request). The pizza shop makes your pizza and delivers it to your house (a response). The web works the same way — you ask for a page, the server sends it to you.

sections.title: What happens when you type google.com?
items: You type 'google.com' in your browser and press Enter.
Your computer looks up where Google lives (like looking up a phone number in a book). This is called DNS.
Your computer connects to Google's server and says 'Can I have your homepage please?'
Google's server sends back all the code for the homepage.
Your browser reads that code and draws the page on your screen.

sections.text: 🤯 Fun Fact: The very first website ever made was by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991. It was just plain text — no pictures, no colors, no videos. You can still visit it today!

terms.definition: Your computer or phone — the thing asking for web pages.
A special computer that stores websites and sends them to you.
When your computer asks for a web page.
When the server sends the web page back to you.
The app you use to view web pages, like Chrome or Firefox.

questions.text: What do we call the computer that stores websites and sends them to you?
questions.explanation: A server is a special computer that stores websites. When you ask for a page, the server sends it to you — just like a pizza shop sends you your pizza!

questions.text: What is YOUR computer called when it asks for a web page?
questions.explanation: Your computer is the client — it makes requests to servers. Think of yourself as the customer at a pizza shop!

questions.text: When you type a website address and press Enter, what is your computer sending to the server?
questions.explanation: Your computer sends a request — it's asking the server 'Hey, can you send me this web page please?'

questions.text: Which app do you use to view websites?
questions.explanation: A browser (like Chrome, Firefox, or Safari) is the app that reads the code sent by the server and shows it as a beautiful web page on your screen.
